'''Stefan Guy Ratchford''' (born 19 July 1988) is an English retired [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] and [[England national rugby league team|England international]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] between 2007 and 2011 in the [[Super League]] and [[RFL Championship|National League One]]. Throughout his career Ratchford played as a {{rlp|fb|wg|ce|so|sh|hk}} and {{rlp|lf}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Stefan Ratchford was born in [[Wigan]], [[Greater Manchester]], England.

He started playing [[rugby league]] at the age of six, initially playing junior rugby for Wigan St Cuthberts before later switching to Wigan St Patricks.<ref name="men">{{cite web|title=Ratchford playing for his Salford future|url=http://www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ratchford-playing-for-his-salford-future-888814|website=Manchester Evening News|access-date=12 December 2017|date=4 May 2010}}</ref>

==Club career==
===Salford Red Devils===
Ratchford signed with [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] in 2005, and made his first team début for the club in 2007 in a fourth round [[Challenge Cup]] match against [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]].<ref name="bbc">{{cite web|title=Warrington Wolves complete Stefan Ratchford signing|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14296075|website=BBC Sport|access-date=12 December 2017|date=27 July 2011}}</ref>

In 2008, following the club's relegation to [[RFL Championship|National League One]], Ratchford established himself as a regular first team player. He formed an effective halfback partnership alongside [[Richie Myler]],<ref name="men" /> helping the team win the [[Northern Rail Cup]], and the Division One Grand Final.

===Warrington Wolves===
[[File:Stefan Ratchford Warrington.jpg|thumb|Ratchford (right) playing for [[Warrington Wolves]] in January 2012]]
Ratchford played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=Guardian UK|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|work=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
 |df=dmy 
}}</ref>

Ratchford played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington"/>

Ratchford played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final |url=http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |publisher=Super League |access-date=14 July 2013 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729050226/http://www.superleague.co.uk/super_league/grandfinal |archive-date=29 July 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|newspaper=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Super League Grand Final: Warrington v Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131007163603/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/0/rugby-league/24339698|url-status=dead|archive-date=7 October 2013|work=BBC Sport|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/10358228/Super-League-Grand-Final-2013-Warrington-Wolves-16-Wigan-Warriors-30-match-report.html|title= Warrington Wolves 16 Wigan Warriors 30|date=5 October 2013|work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

Ratchford signed a new deal in 2014 keeping him at the Warrington club until 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/11488585.Ratchford_pens_new_Wolves_deal_and_eyes_Grand_Final_success|title=Stefan Ratchford has 'unfinished business' at Warrington Wolves|work=Warrington Guardian|date=23 September 2014 }}</ref>

Ratchford played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|access-date=7 November 2019}}</ref>

Ratchford played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
During Warrington's defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] Ratchford was voted winner of the [[Harry Sunderland Trophy]] making him only the fourth recipient of the award to have played for the losing side.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mixed fortunes for Ratchford |date=15 October 2018 |work=[[Rugby Leaguer & League Express]] |issue=3,143 |page=15}}</ref>

Ratchford played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>

Ratchford played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|access-date=1 November 2019}}</ref>
In round 8 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored a try and kicked 11 goals in Warrington's 62–18 victory over Salford.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57246596|title= Salford Red Devils 18-62 Warrington Wolves|work= BBC Sport}}</ref>
Ratchford played 28 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. Ratchford played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 28 March 2024, Ratchford set a new goal kicking record in the Super League converting his 40th goal in a row. Warrington would win the match 58-4 over the hapless [[London Broncos|London]] side.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68569783|title=London Broncos 4-58 Warrington Wolves: Two hat-tricks help Wire return to top spot|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In July 2024, Ratchford suffering a fractured eyesocket and jaw during a game against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] ruling him out for eight weeks.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-07-23 |title=Stefan Ratchford: Warrington Wolves captain out for eight weeks |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cw0yk9x0pywo |access-date=2024-10-18 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref> The injury resulted in him being unable to eat or talk for the first month of his recovery.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2024-09-09 |title=Stefan Ratchford: Warrington Wolves captain reveals injury extent |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/c5ydnql90l6o |access-date=2024-10-18 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, Ratchford played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Ratchford played 19 games for Warrington in the 2025 Super League season as they missed the playoffs finishing 8th on the table. Ratchford announced that he would be retiring following the conclusion of the 2025 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
Ratchford was named in the England squad for [[2009 Four Nations]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby/rugby-league/2009/09/18/salford-s-stefan-ratchford-the-surprise-name-in-england-squad-115875-21682683|title=Salford's Stefan Ratchford the surprise name in England squad|author=mirror Administrator|date=17 September 2009|work=mirror}}</ref> but did not appear in any games during the tournament. At the end of the 2011 season, Ratchford rejected a new contract offer from Salford and joined [[Warrington Wolves]].<ref name="bbc" /> He also made two appearances for the [[England Knights]] in 2011 and 2012.

In October 2017 he was selected in the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup squads#England|England squad]] for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/james-roby-back-fold-wayne-11312505|title=England rugby league World Cup squad revealed - and James Roby is back in the fold|publisher=Mirror|date=9 October 2017|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>

==Personal life==
In 2015, Ratchford set up a business with former Salford City Reds teammate [[Matty Smith (rugby league)|Matty Smith]] called Future Stars, which runs coaching sessions for junior rugby league players.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Wilkinson|first1=Phil|title=Smith: We mean business|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/news/smith-we-mean-business-1-7881821|website=Wigan Today|access-date=3 December 2017|date=28 April 2016}}</ref>
